context: Tests whether the agent gives source-grounded Apple Pay checkout guidance for a SwiftUI physical-goods app.

checklist.name: Capability setup
checklist.description: Covers Apple Pay capability, Merchant ID, payment processing certificate, and merchant entitlement.

checklist.name: Availability checks
checklist.description: Checks both `PKPaymentAuthorizationController.canMakePayments()` and `canMakePayments(usingNetworks:capabilities:)` before offering Apple Pay.

checklist.name: System button
checklist.description: Uses `PayWithApplePayButton` or `PKPaymentButton` and avoids custom Apple Pay logo or text buttons.

checklist.name: Payment request amounts
checklist.description: Builds `PKPaymentRequest`, `PKPaymentSummaryItem`, and `PKShippingMethod` examples with `NSDecimalNumber` money values.

checklist.name: Summary total
checklist.description: Keeps the final `paymentSummaryItems` entry as the merchant or business total shown on the Pay line.

checklist.name: Shipping and contact
checklist.description: Requests relevant shipping or billing contact fields or shipping methods for a physical-goods Apple Pay checkout.

checklist.name: Controller lifetime
checklist.description: Presents with `PKPaymentAuthorizationController`, retains it while visible, assigns the weak delegate, and clears retained state after dismissal or failed presentation.

checklist.name: Authorization processing
checklist.description: Sends `payment.token` or `payment.token.paymentData` to a backend or payment processor and completes with `PKPaymentAuthorizationResult`.

checklist.name: StoreKit boundary
checklist.description: Explains that StoreKit is for digital goods, app features, and digital-content subscriptions while PassKit covers physical goods, real-world services, and donations.
